title: EventData
description: Monadic event payload with three layers: Foundation (raw + seq + session_id) is always present. The agent_payload is the lift — None if unknown agent, Some(typed) if recognized. Each payload contains meta (about the payload: tag, version, timestamp) and content (what happened). SICP 2.4: dispatch on type with tagged data.

raw.description: Original transcript line, never mutated. The foundation. Everything else is derived from this.

seq.description: Sequence number within the translation session.

agent_payload.description: The monadic lift. None (absent) = unknown agent or parse failure — you still have raw. Some = the translator successfully extracted typed fields. Dispatch on meta.agent to determine which variant.

payload_meta.description: The tag. Determined by format auto-detection, not from the transcript. Just enough to dispatch.

agent.description: Discriminant for dispatch. 'claude-code', 'pi-mono', or 'hermes'. This is the SICP type tag.

claude_code_payload.description: Typed extraction for Claude Code events. Foundation → Meta → Content.

is_sidechain.description: Whether this event is from a subagent.

args.description: Tool call arguments — shape varies by tool.

content_types.description: Block types in message (text, tool_use, thinking, etc.).

token_usage.description: Claude Code token usage. Rich caching and inference metadata.

cache_creation.description: Cache creation breakdown by TTL bucket.

parent_tool_use_id.description: Tool use ID this result responds to.

pi_mono_payload.description: Typed extraction for pi-mono (OpenClaw) events. Foundation → Meta → Content.

token_usage.description: Pi-mono token usage. Includes cache and cost data.

tool_call_id.description: ID of the tool call this result responds to.

tool_name.description: Name of tool in result context.

is_error.description: Whether tool result is an error.

first_kept_entry_id.description: First entry ID kept after compaction.

hermes_payload.description: Typed extraction for Hermes Agent (NousResearch/hermes-agent) events. Hermes is provider-polymorphic but stores all messages in OpenAI shape internally. Verified against hermes-agent commit 6e3f7f36 and 5 real sessions against Anthropic claude-sonnet-4-20250514.

seq.description: Plugin-side monotonic per-session sequence number from the input envelope.

timestamp.description: Event timestamp. Plugin path uses UTC-Z (2026-04-10T10:55:02Z). Snapshot path uses naive ISO-8601 (2026-04-10T10:55:02.359248, no timezone).

model.description: Model ID (e.g., claude-sonnet-4-20250514). Present on session_start and available per-turn via hook kwargs.

hermes_version.description: Hermes Agent version (e.g., '0.8.0'). Present on session_start.

system_prompt_preview.description: First 500 chars of system prompt. Present on session_start.

tools.description: Tool names available in the session (e.g., ['read_file', 'write_file', 'terminal', ...]). Present on session_start.

text.description: Message text content. User prompts, assistant responses, and tool result content (which is always a JSON string wrapping structured output — see tool_result_shapes).

stop_reason.description: finish_reason from the assistant message — 'stop' for final text, 'tool_calls' when tools are invoked.

tool.description: Tool name from function.name in the OpenAI tool_calls shape. Hermes tool names: read_file, write_file, search_files, terminal, delegate_task, patch, execute_code, etc.

args.description: Parsed tool arguments. Wire format is a JSON string in function.arguments; the translator parses it into a structured object. Falls back to {"_raw": "..."} if the JSON string is malformed.

tool_use_id.description: Tool call ID from tool_calls[i].id. Anthropic-provider sessions use 'toolu_*' prefix. Links tool_use events to their tool_result.

preceding_text.description: Assistant text content that preceded the tool call in the same turn (eval phase before apply). Null when the assistant message had empty content alongside tool_calls.

tool_call_id.description: On tool result messages: links back to the originating tool_use via tool_calls[i].id. Canonical key — Hermes never uses 'id' as an alias.

tool_name.description: Optional tool name on tool result messages. Verified ABSENT in all 5 real sessions (runtime gap #2 confirmed). The translator reads it defensively but expects null.

reasoning.description: Extended thinking / reasoning content. Top-level string field on assistant messages (not Anthropic-style content blocks — the SDK adapter converts thinking blocks to a flat string before storage). Null when the model does not produce thinking (e.g., Sonnet 4 default mode).

completed.description: Whether the agent loop completed normally (from on_session_end hook).

interrupted.description: Whether the agent loop was interrupted (from on_session_end hook).

message_count.description: Number of messages in the session at the time of the turn-complete event.

hermes_payload.$comment_tool_result_shapes: Hermes tool results always wrap output in a JSON string. Known shapes: read_file → {content, total_lines, file_size, truncated, is_binary, is_image, error?}, write_file → {bytes_written, dirs_created}, terminal → {output, exit_code, error}, search_files → {matches, ...}, delegate_task → {results: [{task_index, status, summary}]}. Errors are encoded INSIDE the content JSON as an 'error' key — there is no separate is_error field on the tool message.
